# Python pass parity

`py_pass_parity_test.go` directly consumes `py_pass_golden.json`. The snapshot
is generated by the real optimizer in
`sonolus.py@1040bc0dcc116efdbca05f144edec302e839bcd3`.

The common canonical form uses reverse-postorder block IDs, first-definition
local/SSA versions, predecessor-ordered Phi arguments, and source-order
expression arguments. `ToSSA` and SCCP snapshots are compared structurally.
`FromSSA` and allocation are compared through final-form invariants because the
Python block-object CFG and Go explicit-edge CFG split Phi critical edges into
different, semantically equivalent block shapes.

Regenerate after deliberately updating the pinned Python baseline:

```powershell
& internal/compiler/optimize/testdata/regenerate.ps1
```

Normal Go tests only consume the checked-in snapshot and do not require Python
or an adjacent checkout.
